zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Lauson George於1603年出生在英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德時，他的父親William Henry George18歲，母親Anne Greene20歲。他於1621年11月1日與Margaret Saward在英國艾可萊特爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1661年5月20日在美國維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特蘭卡斯特享年58歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Lauson George于1603年出生在英国艾可傑爾姆斯福德时，他的父亲William Henry George18岁，母亲Anne Greene20岁。他于1621年11月1日与Margaret Saward在英国艾可萊特爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1661年5月20日在美国弗吉尼亚州蘭卡斯特蘭卡斯特享年58岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州蘭卡斯特。

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Welsh, French, and Romanian: from the personal name George, Latin Georgius, Greek Geōrgios, from an adjectival form, geōrgios ‘rustic’, of Greek geōrgos ‘farmer’. This became established as a personal name in classical times through its association with the fashion for pastoral poetry. Its popularity in western Europe increased at the time of the Crusades, which brought greater contact with the Orthodox Church, in which several Christian martyrs and saints of this name are venerated, in particular a saint believed to have been martyred at Nicomedia in AD 303, who, however, is at best a shadowy figure historically. Nevertheless, by the end of the Middle Ages Saint George had become associated with an unhistorical legend of dragon-slaying exploits, which caught the popular imagination throughout Europe, and he came to be considered the patron saint of England among other places. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Georg , Assyrian/Chaldean Giwargis, Gewargis , or Georgis , and Albanian Gjergji , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Georgiadis , Georgopoulos , Hatzigeorgiou ‘George the Pilgrim’, and Papageorgiou , Romanian Georgescu or Gheorghescu, Serbian Djordjevic . The name George is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Geevarghese and Varghese ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: variant of   Georg  .
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name George (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Djordjevic
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Serbian or Croatian Dragan, Bozidar, Branko, Milan, Miodrag, Momcilo, Predrag, Sinisa, Sotir, Borka, Djordje, Dragana, Dunja, Dusan, Mirko, Miroslav, Milutin, Misha, Vladimir.

Serbian and Croatian (Djordjević, standard spelling Đorđević): patronymic from the especially Serbian personal name Đorđe, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ). Đorđević is the fifth most frequent surname in Serbia. Compare Georgevich .

variants.name: Djukic
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Djukić, standard spelling Đukić): patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Đurađ, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

variants.name: Durko
meanings: Slovak (Ďurko): from the personal name Ďurko, a pet form of Juraj, an equivalent of George . Compare Darko .

variants.name: Gazzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Almando, Attilio, Julio, Salvatore.

Italian: probably from the medieval Greek personal name Gatsos, a pet form of Georgatsos, a derivative of Geōrgios (see George ). Alternatively, a nickname from Sicilian gazzu ‘blue-eyed’ (also ‘short-sighted’), or from Old Italian gazzo ‘deep blue’ (like the feathers of a magpie, Italian gazza). It could also be a habitational name from Gazzo in Padova province, Gazzo Veronese in Verona province, or from a shortened form of Agazzo, from Late Latin Agathius, either a masculine form of Agata or from the analogous ancient Germanic personal name Agathio.

variants.name: Geevarghese
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Geevarghese, a derivative of Greek Geōrgios (see George ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Ganta
meanings: Indian (Andhra Pradesh): name borne by a sept of Kamma (agrarian) and Balija (trader) people, from Telugu ganta ‘bell’.
Romanian (Ganţă): from a derivative of the personal name Gană, Gane, Ganu, of Bulgarian origin (a pet form of local names corresponding to George ).

variants.name: Durica
meanings: Slovak and Czech (Moravian) (Ďurica): patronymic from the personal name Ďuro, a vernacular form of local equivalents (Juraj, Jura) of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: Djuric
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Djurić, standard spelling Đurić): patronymic from the personal names Đura and Đuro, short forms of Đurađ, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ). See also Juric , compare Duric .

variants.name: Georg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerd, Kurt, Manfred.
German: from the personal name Georg, an equivalent of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: Djokic
meanings: Serbian (Djokić, standard spelling Đokić): patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Đorđe, from Greek Geōrgios (see George ).

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

nameMeanings.name: Lauson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a patronymic from Law, a Middle English short form of Laurence . In Australia it has been fairly regularly used as a given name in honour of the explorer William Lawson ( 1774–1850 ) and the writer Henry Lawson ( 1867–1922 ).

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Lauson George was born in 1603, in Chelmsford, Essex, England, his father, William Henry George, was 18 and his mother, Anne Greene, was 20. He married Margaret Saward on 1 November 1621, in Writtle, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 20 May 1661, in Lancaster, Lancaster, Virginia, United States, at the age of 58, and was buried in Lancaster, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Lauson George(이)가 1603년 영국 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Henry George(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Greene(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1621년 11월 1일 영국 에세스 리틀에서 Margaret Saward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1661년 5월 20일 미국 버지니아 주 랭커스터 랭커스터에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 랭커스터에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Lauson George родился в 1603 г. в местности челмсфорд, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени William Henry George было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Greene, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Saward 1 ноября 1621 г. в местности риттл, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 20 мая 1661 г. в местности ланкастир, ланкастир, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности ланкастир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.
